title: ainsliaea henryi var. subalpina
intro: Ainsliaea henryi var. subalpina, also known as the Ainsliaea subalpina, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, native to the mountainous regions of China. The plant has been used in traditional Chinese medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, making it useful for treating conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Ainsliaea subalpina include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and boosting the immune system, as well as its potential to treat certain types of cancer. Herbal preparations of Ainsliaea subalpina include tea, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to treat various health conditions, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
